title: Risk Management Methodology — Capital Preservation Framework
overview: Risk management is the difference between a strategy that survives its drawdowns and one that blows up. Most quant work focuses on entry signals. The pros focus on what happens after entry — sizing, stops, correlation, and kill switches. This methodology is built from a desk running 6 deployed signals with daily regime audits, funding-rate gates, and anti-anchoring rules. The core principle: you don't have an edge if you can't size it. A strategy with Sharpe 2.0 sized at full Kelly will blow up faster than a strategy with Sharpe 0.5 sized at quarter-Kelly. The sizing is the edge. The stops are the edge. The correlation awareness is the edge.
core_methodology: 1. KELLY SIZING: f* = win_rate - (1-win_rate)/(avg_win/avg_loss). Default to half-Kelly (f*/2). Quarter-Kelly when OOS period < 2x in-sample. Never exceed full Kelly. 2. STOP PLACEMENT: Stops go beyond structural levels, not at round numbers. For longs: below support minus 0.5 ATR. For shorts: above resistance plus 0.5 ATR. Max risk per trade = 2% of equity. If stop distance would exceed 2%, reduce position size — NEVER tighten the stop into noise. 3. CORRELATION MATRIX: Effective N = N/(1+(N-1)*avg_correlation). A portfolio of 6 strategies with avg correlation 0.3 is effectively ~3.5 independent bets. Size accordingly. If effective N < N/2, consolidate correlated strategies. 4. DRAWDOWN TRIAGE: Current drawdown > max historical drawdown → CRITICAL — uncharted territory. Trades since peak > 2x avg winning streak AND win rate halved → CRITICAL — edge may have decayed. 5. FUNDING GATE: If funding_erosion_bps > 0.5 * estimated_edge_bps, CLOSE — funding has consumed half the edge. If short and funding is negative, you're getting paid — this is tailwind. 6. PORTFOLIO HEAT: 0.3*(gross_exposure_pct/100) + 0.3*(max_factor_exposure) + 0.4*(largest_position/equity). Heat > 70 → reduce. Heat > 90 → you're running a single bet dressed as diversification.
evaluation_framework: PASS/FAIL: (1) Projected max drawdown at recommended size < 25% — above this, the strategy is uninvestable, (2) No single position > 10% of equity at entry, (3) Portfolio effective N >= 3 — below this, you're not diversified enough to survive one bad correlation event, (4) Every position has a stop beyond a structural level, not at a round number, (5) Funding costs are modeled and gated for every perpetuals position. KILL RULES: Kill/reduce immediately if: (a) current drawdown exceeds max historical drawdown, (b) 2+ correlated strategies are in drawdown simultaneously, (c) funding costs exceed 50% of expected edge, (d) portfolio heat > 90 for more than 2 consecutive days. The desk's anti-anchoring rule (OJ proposal #008): when re-evaluating confidence within 7 days, require new evidence before adjusting. No default +0.01 increments.
implementation_notes: Start with Kelly sizing first — it's the foundation. Then build stops using ATR(14) and structural levels. Then compute the correlation matrix. Then add the funding gate. Then the drawdown triage protocol. Each layer adds protection. Do not skip layers — a strategy with perfect sizing and no stops is just as dangerous as one with stops and no sizing. For agent automation: risk checks should run before every entry (sizing check + stop validation) and daily for open positions (drawdown triage + funding gate + correlation check). The desk's auto-re-audit trigger (OJ proposal #007) fires when sub-regime weights shift by >= 0.05 — this pattern applies to risk re-evaluation too.
common_failure_modes: 1. GAMBLER'S RUIN: Sizing too large relative to edge. Even a +EV strategy goes to zero if sized at full Kelly with a bad streak. Fix: half-Kelly is the default. 2. STOP HUNTING: Placing stops at obvious levels (round numbers, recent lows). Market makers hunt these. Fix: place stops beyond structural levels with a buffer. 3. CORRELATION BLINDNESS: Running 6 strategies that are all long-BTC variants. When BTC drops, all 6 get hit simultaneously. Fix: compute the correlation matrix before sizing. 4. FUNDING BLINDNESS: Holding a long perp position while paying 0.1% funding every 8 hours. Over a week, funding can consume 50%+ of the edge. Fix: the funding gate is mandatory. 5. ANCHORING BIAS: 'The strategy worked yesterday, so it's fine today.' Confidence drifts up without evidence. Fix: anti-anchoring rule — require new data for confidence adjustments. 6. DRAWDOWN DOUBLING: Averaging down into a losing position because 'the thesis is still intact.' The thesis is the market's opinion, not yours. Fix: the drawdown triage protocol is non-negotiable.
regime_considerations: TRENDING UP: Long positions have tailwind. Shorts are fighting the trend — reduce short size by 50% or avoid entirely. Stops: wider for longs (trend pullbacks are normal), tight for shorts (counter-trend rallies are violent). TRENDING DOWN: Inverse of above. RANGING: Mean-reversion sizing works. Trend-following sizing fails — you're buying tops and selling bottoms. Reduce all position sizes by 25% — range breakouts are false 70% of the time. HIGH VOL: Reduce all position sizes by 30-50%. Widen all stops by 1.5x. The Kelly formula overestimates optimal size in high vol because it assumes stable win rates — the win rate drops in high vol for almost every strategy. LOW VOL: Normal sizing. Be patient — low vol precedes expansion. The risk is overtrading during compression, not the sizing itself. PORTFOLIO-LEVEL: When regimes are mixed across instruments (BTC trending, SOL ranging), the portfolio heat score captures this automatically — the max_factor_exposure term will be lower because returns are less correlated.

prompts.title: Kelly Position Sizer
prompts.prompt: Given a strategy's historical win_rate and avg_win_bps/avg_loss_bps, compute optimal Kelly fraction: f* = win_rate - (1-win_rate)/(avg_win/avg_loss). Apply: half-Kelly (f*/2), quarter-Kelly (f*/4 — when OOS < 2x in-sample), max_drawdown_constraint (if projected DD > 25%, reduce further). Output: full_kelly, half_kelly, quarter_kelly, recommended_fraction, projected_max_drawdown.

prompts.title: Correlation Matrix Risk Check
prompts.prompt: Given N active strategies with daily PnL, compute: NxN correlation matrix, flag pairs >0.5 as 'highly_correlated', effective_N = N/(1+(N-1)*avg_correlation). If effective_N < N/2 → 'over-concentrated' — consolidate correlated strategies. Compute portfolio-level max drawdown.

prompts.title: Stop-Loss Placement Engine
prompts.prompt: Given entry_price, current_price, instrument, ATR(14), nearest_support, nearest_resistance, direction. Rules: stop beyond structural level + 0.5 ATR. Max risk = 2% equity. If stop distance exceeds 2%, reduce size — never tighten into noise. If price has moved favorably and stop can go to breakeven while staying beyond structure, recommend trailing. Output: stop_price, risk_percent, size_recommendation.

prompts.title: Drawdown Triage Protocol
prompts.prompt: Given current_drawdown_pct, max_historical_drawdown_pct, trades_since_peak, avg_win_rate, regime_at_drawdown_start. Rules: if current > max_historical → CRITICAL (uncharted territory). If trades_since_peak > 2x avg winning streak AND win_rate halved → CRITICAL (edge decay). If regime mismatch explains drawdown → cap at 'concerning' (edge may be intact in wrong regime). Output: severity, action, reasoning.

prompts.title: Funding Rate Gate
prompts.prompt: Given perp position (instrument, direction, entry_funding, current_funding, avg_funding_7d, duration_hours). Compute funding_erosion_bps. If duration > 24h AND current_funding > avg_7d * 1.5 AND long → WARN. If funding_erosion > 0.5 * estimated_edge → CLOSE (funding consumed half the edge). If short AND funding negative → PASS (getting paid). Output: funding_erosion_bps, edge_remaining_pct, gate_decision.

prompts.title: Portfolio Heat Map
prompts.prompt: Given portfolio of active positions with risk_factors, compute: gross_exposure_pct, net_directional_exposure, top 3 risk factors with concentration warnings, correlated_exposure flags, portfolio_heat = 0.3*(gross/100) + 0.3*(max_factor) + 0.4*(largest_position/equity). Heat > 70 → reduce. Heat > 90 → single bet dressed as diversification — consolidate.
